description: Significantly increases bleeding risk. Both affect blood clotting through different mechanisms. Concurrent use requires close medical supervision.
NSAIDs increase bleeding risk and may interfere with warfarin metabolism. Can cause serious gastrointestinal bleeding.
Naproxen increases bleeding risk when combined with warfarin. May cause GI bleeding.
Fluconazole inhibits warfarin metabolism leading to dangerously elevated anticoagulant effects.
Metronidazole may increase warfarin effect and bleeding risk. Monitor INR closely.
Amiodarone significantly increases warfarin levels. Dose adjustment and close INR monitoring required.
Combined anticoagulant and antiplatelet effect greatly increases bleeding risk.
Ibuprofen may reduce the cardioprotective effect of aspirin. Take aspirin at least 30 minutes before ibuprofen.
Both are antiplatelet agents. Combination increases bleeding risk but may be prescribed together under supervision.
Concurrent NSAID use increases risk of GI bleeding and ulcers.
Major increase in bleeding risk. Both affect clotting mechanisms.
Alcohol increases the risk of lactic acidosis with metformin. Avoid excessive alcohol consumption.
Furosemide may increase metformin levels. Monitor blood sugar and kidney function.
Ciprofloxacin may alter blood sugar levels when used with metformin. Monitor glucose closely.
Metformin should be stopped before and after contrast media procedures due to lactic acidosis risk.
Amiodarone increases simvastatin levels significantly raising risk of muscle damage (rhabdomyolysis).
Fluconazole inhibits statin metabolism leading to elevated levels and muscle toxicity risk.
Diltiazem increases simvastatin levels. Maximum simvastatin dose should be limited.
Verapamil increases simvastatin levels. Dose limitation recommended.
Fluconazole may increase statin levels. Monitor for muscle pain or weakness.
Ketoconazole significantly increases atorvastatin levels risking rhabdomyolysis.
Amlodipine increases simvastatin levels. Simvastatin dose should not exceed 20mg.
ACE inhibitors can increase potassium levels. Adding potassium supplements may cause dangerous hyperkalemia.
Both increase potassium levels. Risk of hyperkalemia when combined.
Combined use increases risk of hyperkalemia. Monitor potassium levels regularly.
Risk of hyperkalemia. Monitor potassium levels closely.
Amiodarone increases digoxin levels by 70-100%. Dose reduction and monitoring required.
Verapamil increases digoxin levels and both slow heart rate. Risk of severe bradycardia.
Furosemide-induced low potassium increases digoxin toxicity risk. Monitor electrolytes.
Thiazide-induced hypokalemia increases risk of digoxin toxicity.
NSAIDs reduce lithium excretion causing toxic lithium levels. Avoid combination.
Naproxen reduces lithium clearance. Risk of lithium toxicity.
Furosemide reduces lithium excretion. Risk of lithium toxicity.
Thiazide diuretics reduce lithium excretion and can cause toxicity.
ACE inhibitors may increase lithium levels. Monitor lithium levels closely.
Risk of serotonin syndrome. Both increase serotonin levels. Symptoms include agitation and high fever.
Never combine two SSRIs. Extreme risk of serotonin syndrome.
Risk of serotonin syndrome. Avoid concurrent use or use with extreme caution.
Potentially fatal serotonin syndrome. Must have washout period between medications.
Increased risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures.
Both increase serotonin. High risk of serotonin syndrome.
Risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures with concurrent use.
Fluoxetine increases amitriptyline levels. Risk of enhanced side effects.
NSAIDs reduce methotrexate clearance causing toxic accumulation. Can be fatal.
Naproxen reduces methotrexate clearance. Risk of severe toxicity.
Aspirin may increase methotrexate levels. Use with caution and monitoring.
Calcium significantly reduces ciprofloxacin absorption. Take at least 2 hours apart.
Iron reduces fluoroquinolone absorption. Separate doses by at least 2 hours.
Ciprofloxacin increases theophylline levels. Risk of theophylline toxicity.
Ciprofloxacin dramatically increases tizanidine levels. Combination is contraindicated.
Calcium reduces levothyroxine absorption. Take levothyroxine 4 hours before calcium.
Iron reduces levothyroxine absorption. Separate by at least 4 hours.
PPIs may reduce levothyroxine absorption. May need dose adjustment.
Omeprazole significantly reduces clopidogrel effectiveness. Use pantoprazole instead if PPI needed.
Pantoprazole has less interaction with clopidogrel compared to omeprazole but some reduction in effect possible.
Carbamazepine reduces effectiveness of hormonal contraceptives. Use alternative contraception.
Complex interaction. Carbamazepine reduces valproate levels while valproate increases carbamazepine toxic metabolite.
Fluconazole increases carbamazepine levels. Monitor for toxicity.
Valproate displaces phenytoin from protein binding. Free phenytoin levels increase.
Fluconazole increases phenytoin levels. Monitor for toxicity signs.
Phenytoin reduces hormonal contraceptive effectiveness significantly.
Potentially fatal drop in blood pressure. Combination is absolutely contraindicated.
Enhanced blood pressure lowering effect. Risk of hypotension and dizziness.
Combined CNS depression. Risk of excessive sedation and respiratory depression.
Combined CNS depression and increased seizure risk.
Combined CNS and respiratory depression. Potentially fatal.
Extreme risk of respiratory depression. Combination can be fatal.
Combined respiratory and CNS depression. Life-threatening interaction.
Enhanced CNS depression. Increased risk of sedation and respiratory depression.
Combined CNS depression and increased seizure risk.
Increased risk of gastrointestinal bleeding and ulceration.
Combined use increases GI bleeding risk. Use gastroprotection.
Corticosteroids may alter warfarin response. Monitor INR.
Both increase potassium levels. High risk of dangerous hyperkalemia.
Allopurinol markedly increases azathioprine toxicity. Dose must be reduced 75%.
Fluconazole increases colchicine levels. Risk of colchicine toxicity.
Both prokinetic agents. Combined use increases risk of cardiac arrhythmias.
Combined use can cause severe bradycardia and heart block.
Risk of severe bradycardia and cardiac arrest.
Combined negative effects on heart rate and conduction. Risk of cardiac arrest.
Risk of severe bradycardia and heart block.
Enhanced heart rate slowing. Monitor for bradycardia.
